summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #289: the framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine (composite of a date text input + Calendar in a popover), owning open/close, selection, input parse/validation, and ARIA/data attributes ŌĆö agnostic core only, adapters out of scope.
summary.outcome: DatePicker state machine fully implemented and green (109 tests: unit + ARIA + 27 snapshots + spec-conformance + proptest at 2000 cases; 100% function / 99.84% line coverage). Along the way: 9 spec drift bugs fixed and synced, a silent xtask snapshot-count gate bug fixed (two whole categories were skipped), a pre-existing rating_group snapshot shortfall resolved, and DateField + DatePicker locale ordering refactored to use the already-complete but private `date_order()` in ars-i18n. Four commits created and xci-fast passed.

repo: The `Machine` trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` ŌĆö typed enum, not string names. Effects must be emitted via `PendingEffect::named(Effect::Variant)`; `use_platform_effects()` / DOM focus calls must never appear in agnostic core. Adapters execute focus from the named intent.

`CalendarDate` fields are `pub(crate)`. Public accessors are `year()->i32`, `month()->u8`, `day()->u8` ŌĆö no `NonZero`, no `.get()`. `new_gregorian(i32, u8, u8) -> Result<ŌĆ”>` ŌĆö use `.ok()` to convert. The helper `nzu8` referenced in the spec does not exist anywhere.
`CalendarDate` has no `PartialOrd`/`Ord`. Min/max range checks must use `date.compare(m) -> Ordering` (comparing `!= Less` / `!= Greater`).
`ConnectApi` is a 2-method trait: only `type Part` and `fn part_attrs` are required.
`ComponentIds` exposes no public `base_id` field ŌĆö the accessor is `ids.id()`.

`Context` should derive only `#[derive(Clone)]` with a manual `impl Debug` that skips `Messages` ŌĆö matching the Calendar convention. Adding `PartialEq` fails because `Messages` (holds `MessageFn`/`Arc`) is not `PartialEq`.
`Service::new` takes props and environment by reference (`&Env`, `&Messages`), not by value. `send` is `#[must_use]` ŌĆö callers must `drop(svc.send(ŌĆ”))` or bind the result.

`is_date_unavailable` in Calendar props is `Option<calendar::IsDateUnavailableFn>` (a `Callback<dyn Fn(&CalendarDate)->bool + Send+Sync>`), not a bare `fn` pointer. DatePicker must reuse this type to forward the predicate cleanly.

The xtask snapshot-count gate (`is_component_category`) matched hyphenated directory names (`date-time`, `data-display`) while source dirs and snapshot file segments use underscores (`date_time`, `data_display`). This silently excluded two entire categories from the gate for all merged PRs.

`ars-i18n` already contained a complete `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` function (CLDR via ICU4X under `--features i18n`, JS `Intl.DateTimeFormat().formatToParts()` on wasm, `(language, region)` heuristic fallback) ŌĆö it was just private. Both DateField and DatePicker had reimplemented only its fallback heuristic independently, causing potential drift.

`rating_group` (merged #694) was sitting at 8 snapshots against a gate floor of 9 ŌĆö only discovered after the category-recognition bug was fixed. The missing snapshot was the 'unhighlighted item' branch (item index beyond the default value).

A `Bindable` wrapping a controlled prop exposes an `internal` value via `sync_controlled(None)`. If `internal` is never updated by `set()` during controlled-open transitions, a controlledŌåÆuncontrolled prop flip reveals a stale value. The correct fix is to make `State` the single source of truth and remove the redundant `open` Bindable from `Context` entirely ŌĆö the `Api` reads `is_open()` from State, never the Bindable.

code.finding: `is_component_category` matched hyphenated names (`date-time`) but source dirs and snapshot segments use underscores (`date_time`). Fixed by also matching underscored variants. A regression test was added to lock both forms.

`date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` ŌĆö private function with CLDR (icu4x probe-date), JS `Intl` (wasm), and `(language, region)` fallback paths already fully implemented. Existing tests at lines 2559-2571 cover en-USŌåÆMDY, en-CAŌåÆYMD, de-DEŌåÆDMY, ja-JPŌåÆYMD. Made public and exported in this PR.

New file. Key design: `State` is the single source of truth for open/closed (no `open` Bindable in Context). Named `Effect` enum (`FocusCalendar`, `RestoreFocusToTrigger`, `RestoreFocusToInput`). `default_format_for_locale` delegates to `ars_i18n::date_order()` for field order. `SyncProps` event + `on_props_changed` + `initial_effects` implemented per sibling convention.

`segment_order_for_locale` previously contained a hand-rolled `(language, region)` heuristic table. Refactored to delegate to `ars_i18n::date_order()` so DateField and DatePicker are guaranteed to agree on locale field order.
Added `rating_group_item_unhighlighted` snapshot (item index 4 in a 5-item group with default value 2.0) to bring snapshot count from 8 to 9, satisfying the gate floor.

workflow: TDD discipline: write all tests first (watching them fail), then implement. Proptest should run with elevated case counts (2000+) during audit ŌĆö it caught a real architectural bug (Bindable/State divergence) that unit tests missed.
`cargo insta accept` does not accept a `-p` package flag ŌĆö it must be run at workspace root and accepts all pending snapshots globally.

Spec-to-code drift in illustrative ┬¦1.7 sections is a recurring source of compile-blockers. Always grep the real source for API shapes before trusting spec code blocks; the spec is authoritative for *contract* but illustrative snippets often lag implementation.

The post-implementation-audit skill (spec drift ŌåÆ coverage ŌåÆ completeness rounds) caught 4+ spec drift issues, the xtask gate bug, the rating_group floor miss, and coverage gaps in `part_attrs` dispatch and malformed-format fallback paths ŌĆö all landed in the same PR.
Multi-commit history (4 logical units: drive-by fix ŌåÆ i18n expose ŌåÆ xtask/snapshot ŌåÆ main feature) makes large PRs reviewable. Stage files selectively rather than `git add -A`.
Pre-existing staged changes from before the session can break xci-fast (nightly fmt check). Always `git status` before committing to catch stale staged state from other contributors.

When an ars-i18n function already solves a problem but is private, expose it rather than adding a redundant `IntlBackend` trait method. The backend-selection logic (CLDR/wasm/fallback) is already wired; exposing the function costs one visibility change and a re-export.

friction: Spec ┬¦1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers vs the real codebase: no `type Effect`, string effect names, `CalendarDate` field access instead of accessors, nonexistent `nzu8` helper, `NonZero` usage, `>=`/`<=` on a non-`Ord` type, missing `ids.id()`, bare `fn` instead of `IsDateUnavailableFn`, by-value `Service::new`, and omitted `on_props_changed`/`initial_effects`. The spec's 'Spec impact: No spec change required' claim was inaccurate.
An Explore subagent guessed the DatePicker API (events like `InputChange`, fields like `input_text`) rather than reading the spec ŌĆö those guesses had to be discarded and the spec read directly.

Proptest found a real bug mid-implementation (open Bindable diverging from State when controlled props changed), requiring an architectural change (removing the Bindable entirely). A second proptest invariant was itself incorrectly written and also had to be revised.
`cargo insta accept -p ars-components` failed ŌĆö the `-p` flag is not supported; had to accept all pending snapshots globally.

A stale staged change in `pagination/mod.rs` (from before the session, by another contributor) blocked xci-fast on the nightly fmt check. Required diagnosing and applying the fmt fix before commits could proceed.
Coverage tooling required a separate `llvm-cov` invocation to find uncovered lines; the output format needed multiple bash-extraction passes to isolate date_picker-specific gaps.
open_items: The `js_date_order` wasm path in `ars-i18n` (JS `Intl.DateTimeFormat().formatToParts()`) was exposed but not exercised in this PR ŌĆö nightly wasm-coverage CI is the only gate that fully validates it.
Date separator derivation (`.` for de-DE, `/` otherwise) is still a small heuristic in DatePicker, not CLDR-backed. Only field order was moved to `date_order()`.
Pre-existing `utility/highlight` snapshot orphans still block a bare `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` run ŌĆö unrelated to this PR but will affect the next contributor who runs that command.
94 pre-existing crate-wide doc warnings exist in ars-components; none introduced by this PR but the baseline is noisy.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for DatePicker are separate tasks (explicitly out of scope for #289).

The one genuinely unreachable line (`Event::SyncProps(_) => None` exhaustiveness arm, line 743 of date_picker/mod.rs) reduces line coverage to 99.84% ŌĆö a known and accepted gap, same pattern as Calendar.
Full `cargo xci` (not just `xci-fast`) should be run at some point to exercise the wasm-coverage and feature-matrix groups that validate the `web-intl` path wired in this PR.
